aiTitle: Verify repository setup for Claude Code

content.content:
1	# Code Patterns & Structure
2	
3	> **Part of:** [terraform-skill](../SKILL.md)
4	> **Purpose:** Comprehensive patterns for Terraform/OpenTofu code structure and modern features
5	
6	This document provides detailed code patterns, structure guidelines, and modern Terraform features. For high-level principles, see the [main skill file](../SKILL.md).
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Table of Contents
11	
12	1. [Block Ordering & Structure](#block-ordering--structure)
13	2. [Count vs For_Each Deep Dive](#count-vs-for_each-deep-dive)
14	3. [Modern Terraform Features (1.0+)](#modern-terraform-features-10)
15	4. [Version Management](#version-management)
16	5. [Refactoring Patterns](#refactoring-patterns)
17	6. [Locals for Dependency Management](#locals-for-dependency-management)
18	
19	---
20	
21	## Block Ordering & Structure
22	
23	### Resource Block Structure
24	
25	**Strict argument ordering:**
26	
27	1. `count` or `for_each` FIRST (blank line after)
28	2. Other arguments (alphabetical or logical grouping)
29	3. `tags` as last real argument
30	4. `depends_on` after tags (if needed)
31	5. `lifecycle` at the very end (if needed)
32	
33	```hcl
34	# ✅ GOOD - Correct ordering
35	resource "aws_nat_gateway" "this" {
36	  count = var.create_nat_gateway ? 1 : 0
37	
38	  allocation_id = aws_eip.this[0].id
39	  subnet_id     = aws_subnet.public[0].id
40	
41	  tags = {
42	    Name        = "${var.name}-nat"
43	    Environment = var.environment
44	  }
45	
46	  depends_on = [aws_internet_gateway.this]
47	
48	  lifecycle {
49	    create_before_destroy = true
50	  }
51	}
52	
53	# ❌ BAD - Wrong ordering
54	resource "aws_nat_gateway" "this" {
55	  allocation_id = aws_eip.this[0].id
56	
57	  tags = { Name = "nat" }
58	
59	  count = var.create_nat_gateway ? 1 : 0  # Should be first
60	
61	  subnet_id = aws_subnet.public[0].id
62	
63	  lifecycle {
64	    create_before_destroy = true
65	  }
66	
67	  depends_on = [aws_internet_gateway.this]  # Should be after tags
68	}
69	```
70	
71	### Variable Definition Structure
72	
73	**Variable block ordering:**
74	
75	1. `description` (ALWAYS required)
76	2. `type`
77	3. `default`
78	4. `sensitive` (when setting to true)
79	5. `nullable` (when setting to false)
80	6. `validation`
81	
82	```hcl
83	# ✅ GOOD - Correct ordering and structure
84	variable "environment" {
85	  description = "Environment name for resource tagging"
86	  type        = string
87	  default     = "dev"
88	  nullable    = false
89	
90	  validation {
91	    condition     = contains(["dev", "staging", "prod"], var.environment)
92	    error_message = "Environment must be one of: dev, staging, prod."
93	  }
94	}
95	```
96	
97	### Variable Type Preferences
98	
99	- Prefer **simple types** (`string`, `number`, `list()`, `map()`) over `object()` unless strict validation needed
100	- Use `optional()` for optional object attributes (Terraform 1.3+)
101	- Use `any` to disable validation at certain depths or support multiple types
102	
103	**Modern variable patterns (Terraform 1.3+):**
104	
105	```hcl
106	# ✅ GOOD - Using optional() for object attributes
107	variable "database_config" {
108	  description = "Database configuration with optional parameters"
109	  type = object({
110	    name               = string
111	    engine             = string
112	    instance_class     = string
113	    backup_retention   = optional(number, 7)      # Default: 7
114	    monitoring_enabled = optional(bool, true)     # Default: true
115	    tags               = optional(map(string), {}) # Default: {}
116	  })
117	}
118	
119	# Usage - only required fields needed
120	database_config = {
121	  name           = "mydb"
122	  engine         = "mysql"
123	  instance_class = "db.t3.micro"
124	  # Optional fields use defaults
125	}
126	```
127	
128	**Complex type example:**
129	
130	```hcl
131	# For lists/maps of same type
132	variable "subnet_configs" {
133	  description = "Map of subnet configurations"
134	  type        = map(map(string))  # All values are maps of strings
135	}
136	
137	# When types vary, use any
138	variable "mixed_config" {
139	  description = "Configuration with varying types"
140	  type        = any
141	}
142	```
143	
144	### Output Structure
145	
146	**Pattern:** `{name}_{type}_{attribute}`
147	
148	```hcl
149	# ✅ GOOD
150	output "security_group_id" {  # "this_" should be omitted
151	  description = "The ID of the security group"
152	  value       = try(aws_security_group.this[0].id, "")
153	}
154	
155	output "private_subnet_ids" {  # Plural for list
156	  description = "List of private subnet IDs"
157	  value       = aws_subnet.private[*].id
158	}
159	
160	# ❌ BAD
161	output "this_security_group_id" {  # Don't prefix with "this_"
162	  value = aws_security_group.this[0].id
163	}
164	
165	output "subnet_id" {  # Should be plural "subnet_ids"
166	  value = aws_subnet.private[*].id  # Returns list
167	}
168	```
169	
170	---
171	
172	## Count vs For_Each Deep Dive
173	
174	### When to use count
175	
176	✓ **Simple numeric replication:**
177	```hcl
178	resource "aws_subnet" "public" {
179	  count = 3
180	
181	  cidr_block = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, count.index)
182	}
183	```
184	
185	✓ **Boolean conditions (create or don't):**
186	```hcl
187	# ✅ GOOD - Boolean condition
188	resource "aws_nat_gateway" "this" {
189	  count = var.create_nat_gateway ? 1 : 0
190	}
191	
192	# Less preferred - length check
193	resource "aws_nat_gateway" "this" {
194	  count = length(var.public_subnets) > 0 ? 1 : 0
195	}
196	```
197	
198	✓ **When order doesn't matter and items won't change**
199	
200	### When to use for_each
201	
202	✓ **Reference resources by key:**
203	```hcl
204	resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
205	  for_each = toset(var.availability_zones)
206	
207	  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.this.id
208	  availability_zone = each.key
209	  cidr_block        = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 4, index(var.availability_zones, each.key))
210	}
211	
212	# Reference by key: aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"]
213	```
214	
215	✓ **Items may be added/removed from middle:**
216	```hcl
217	# ❌ BAD with count - removing middle item recreates all subsequent resources
218	resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
219	  count = length(var.availability_zones)
220	
221	  availability_zone = var.availability_zones[count.index]
222	  # If var.availability_zones[1] removed, all resources after recreated!
223	}
224	
225	# ✅ GOOD with for_each - removal only affects that one resource
226	resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
227	  for_each = toset(var.availability_zones)
228	
229	  availability_zone = each.key
230	  # Removing one AZ only destroys that subnet
231	}
232	```
233	
234	✓ **Creating multiple named resources:**
235	```hcl
236	variable "environments" {
237	  default = {
238	    dev = {
239	      instance_type = "t3.micro"
240	      instance_count = 1
241	    }
242	    prod = {
243	      instance_type = "t3.large"
244	      instance_count = 3
245	    }
246	  }
247	}
248	
249	resource "aws_instance" "app" {
250	  for_each = var.environments
251	
252	  instance_type = each.value.instance_type
253	  count         = each.value.instance_count
254	
255	  tags = {
256	    Environment = each.key  # "dev" or "prod"
257	  }
258	}
259	```
260	
261	### Count to For_Each Migration
262	
263	**When to migrate:** When you need stable resource addressing or items might be added/removed from middle of list.
264	
265	**Migration steps:**
266	
267	1. Add `for_each` to resource
268	2. Use `moved` blocks to preserve existing resources
269	3. Remove `count` after verifying with `terraform plan`
270	
271	**Complete example:**
272	
273	```hcl
274	# Before (using count)
275	variable "availability_zones" {
276	  default = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"]
277	}
278	
279	resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
280	  count = length(var.availability_zones)
281	
282	  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.this.id
283	  cidr_block        = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, count.index)
284	  availability_zone = var.availability_zones[count.index]
285	
286	  tags = {
287	    Name = "private-${var.availability_zones[count.index]}"
288	  }
289	}
290	
291	# Reference: aws_subnet.private[0].id
292	
293	# After (using for_each)
294	resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
295	  for_each = toset(var.availability_zones)
296	
297	  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.this.id
298	  cidr_block        = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, index(var.availability_zones, each.key))
299	  availability_zone = each.key
300	
301	  tags = {
302	    Name = "private-${each.key}"
303	  }
304	}
305	
306	# Reference: aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"].id
307	
308	# Migration blocks (prevents resource recreation)
309	moved {
310	  from = aws_subnet.private[0]
311	  to   = aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"]
312	}
313	
314	moved {
315	  from = aws_subnet.private[1]
316	  to   = aws_subnet.private["us-east-1b"]
317	}
318	
319	moved {
320	  from = aws_subnet.private[2]
321	  to   = aws_subnet.private["us-east-1c"]
322	}
323	
324	# Verify migration:
325	# terraform plan should show "moved" operations, not destroy/create
326	```
327	
328	**Benefits after migration:**
329	- Removing "us-east-1b" only destroys that subnet (not c)
330	- Adding new AZ doesn't affect existing subnets
331	- Resources have stable addresses by AZ name
332	
333	---
334	
335	## Modern Terraform Features (1.0+)
336	
337	### try() Function (Terraform 0.13+)
338	
339	**Use try() instead of element(concat()):**
340	
341	```hcl
342	# ✅ GOOD - Modern try() function
343	output "security_group_id" {
344	  description = "The ID of the security group"
345	  value       = try(aws_security_group.this[0].id, "")
346	}
347	
348	output "first_subnet_id" {
349	  description = "ID of first subnet with multiple fallbacks"
350	  value       = try(
351	    aws_subnet.public[0].id,
352	    aws_subnet.private[0].id,
353	    ""
354	  )
355	}
356	
357	# ❌ BAD - Legacy pattern
358	output "security_group_id" {
359	  value = element(concat(aws_security_group.this.*.id, [""]), 0)
360	}
361	```
362	
363	### nullable = false (Terraform 1.1+)
364	
365	**Set nullable = false for non-null variables:**
366	
367	```hcl
368	# ✅ GOOD (Terraform 1.1+)
369	variable "vpc_cidr" {
370	  description = "CIDR block for VPC"
371	  type        = string
372	  nullable    = false  # Passing null uses default, not null
373	  default     = "10.0.0.0/16"
374	}
375	```
376	
377	### optional() with Defaults (Terraform 1.3+)
378	
379	**Use optional() for object attributes:**
380	
381	```hcl
382	# ✅ GOOD - Using optional() for object attributes
383	variable "database_config" {
384	  description = "Database configuration with optional parameters"
385	  type = object({
386	    name               = string
387	    engine             = string
388	    instance_class     = string
389	    backup_retention   = optional(number, 7)      # Default: 7
390	    monitoring_enabled = optional(bool, true)     # Default: true
391	    tags               = optional(map(string), {}) # Default: {}
392	  })
393	}
394	
395	# Usage - only required fields needed
396	database_config = {
397	  name           = "mydb"
398	  engine         = "mysql"
399	  instance_class = "db.t3.micro"
400	  # Optional fields use defaults
401	}
402	```
403	
404	### Moved Blocks (Terraform 1.1+)
405	
406	**Rename resources without destroy/recreate:**
407	
408	```hcl
409	# Rename a resource
410	moved {
411	  from = aws_instance.web_server
412	  to   = aws_instance.web
413	}
414	
415	# Rename a module
416	moved {
417	  from = module.old_module_name
418	  to   = module.new_module_name
419	}
420	
421	# Move resource into for_each
422	moved {
423	  from = aws_subnet.private[0]
424	  to   = aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"]
425	}
426	```
427	
428	### Provider-Defined Functions (Terraform 1.8+)
429	
430	**Use provider-specific functions for data transformation:**
431	
432	```hcl
433	# AWS provider function example
434	data "aws_region" "current" {}
435	
436	locals {
437	  # Provider function (Terraform 1.8+)
438	  bucket_name = provider::aws::arn_build("s3", "my-bucket", data.aws_region.current.name)
439	}
440	
441	# Check provider documentation for available functions
442	# Common providers adding functions: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud
443	```
444	
445	### Cross-Variable Validation (Terraform 1.9+)
446	
447	**Reference other variables in validation blocks:**
448	
449	```hcl
450	variable "instance_type" {
451	  description = "EC2 instance type"
452	  type        = string
453	}
454	
455	variable "storage_size" {
456	  description = "Storage size in GB"
457	  type        = number
458	
459	  validation {
460	    # Can reference var.instance_type in Terraform 1.9+
461	    condition = !(
462	      var.instance_type == "db.t3.micro" &&
463	      var.storage_size > 1000
464	    )
465	    error_message = "Micro instances cannot have storage > 1000 GB"
466	  }
467	}
468	
469	variable "environment" {
470	  description = "Environment name"
471	  type        = string
472	}
473	
474	variable "backup_retention" {
475	  description = "Backup retention period in days"
476	  type        = number
477	
478	  validation {
479	    # Production requires longer retention
480	    condition = (
481	      var.environment == "prod" ? var.backup_retention >= 7 : true
482	    )
483	    error_message = "Production environment requires backup_retention >= 7 days"
484	  }
485	}
486	```
487	
488	### Write-Only Arguments (Terraform 1.11+)
489	
490	**Always use write-only arguments or external secret management:**
491	
492	```hcl
493	# ✅ GOOD - External secret with write-only argument
494	data "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_password" {
495	  name = "prod-database-password"
496	}
497	
498	data "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password" {
499	  secret_id = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password.id
500	}
501	
502	resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
503	  engine         = "mysql"
504	  instance_class = "db.t3.micro"
505	  username       = "admin"
506	
507	  # write-only: Terraform sends to AWS then forgets it (not in state)
508	  password_wo = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret_version.db_password.secret_string
509	}
510	
511	# ❌ BAD - Secret ends up in state file
512	resource "random_password" "db" {
513	  length = 16
514	}
515	
516	resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
517	  password = random_password.db.result  # Stored in state!
518	}
519	
520	# ❌ BAD - Variable secret stored in state
521	resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
522	  password = var.db_password  # Ends up in state file
523	}
524	```
525	
526	---
527	
528	## Version Management
529	
530	### Version Constraint Syntax
531	
532	```hcl
533	# Exact version (avoid unless necessary - inflexible)
534	version = "5.0.0"
535	
536	# Pessimistic constraint (recommended for stability)
537	# Allows patch updates only
538	version = "~> 5.0"      # Allows 5.0.x (any x), but not 5.1.0
539	version = "~> 5.0.1"    # Allows 5.0.x where x >= 1, but not 5.1.0
540	
541	# Range constraints
542	version = ">= 5.0, < 6.0"     # Any 5.x version
543	version = ">= 5.0.0, < 5.1.0" # Specific minor version range
544	
545	# Minimum version
546	version = ">= 5.0"  # Any version 5.0 or higher (risky - breaking changes)
547	
548	# Latest (avoid in production - unpredictable)
549	# No version specified = always use latest available
550	```
551	
552	### Versioning Strategy by Component
553	
554	**Terraform itself:**
555	```hcl
556	# versions.tf
557	terraform {
558	  # Pin to minor version, allow patch updates
559	  required_version = "~> 1.9"  # Allows 1.9.x
560	}
561	```
562	
563	**Providers:**
564	```hcl
565	# versions.tf
566	terraform {
567	  required_providers {
568	    aws = {
569	      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
570	      version = "~> 5.0"  # Pin major version, allow minor/patch updates
571	    }
572	    random = {
573	      source  = "hashicorp/random"
574	      version = "~> 3.5"
575	    }
576	  }
577	}
578	```
579	
580	**Modules:**
581	```hcl
582	# Production - pin exact version
583	module "vpc" {
584	  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
585	  version = "5.1.2"  # Exact version for production stability
586	}
587	
588	# Development - allow flexibility
589	module "vpc" {
590	  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
591	  version = "~> 5.1"  # Allow patch updates in dev
592	}
593	```
594	
595	### Update Strategy
596	
597	**Security patches:**
598	- Update immediately
599	- Test in dev → stage → prod
600	- Prioritize provider and Terraform core updates
601	
602	**Minor versions:**
603	- Regular maintenance windows (monthly/quarterly)
604	- Review changelog for breaking changes
605	- Test thoroughly before production
606	
607	**Major versions:**
608	- Planned upgrade cycles
609	- Dedicated testing period
610	- May require code changes
611	- Update in phases: dev → stage → prod
612	
613	### Version Management Workflow
614	
615	```hcl
616	# Step 1: Lock versions in versions.tf
617	terraform {
618	  required_version = "~> 1.9"
619	
620	  required_providers {
621	    aws = {
622	      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
623	      version = "~> 5.0"
624	    }
625	  }
626	}
627	
628	# Step 2: Generate lock file (commit this)
629	terraform init
630	# Creates .terraform.lock.hcl with exact versions used
631	
632	# Step 3: Update providers when needed
633	terraform init -upgrade
634	# Updates to latest within constraints
635	
636	# Step 4: Review and test changes before committing
637	terraform plan
638	```
639	
640	### Example versions.tf Template
641	
642	```hcl
643	terraform {
644	  # Terraform version
645	  required_version = "~> 1.9"
646	
647	  # Provider versions
648	  required_providers {
649	    aws = {
650	      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
651	      version = "~> 5.0"
652	    }
653	    random = {
654	      source  = "hashicorp/random"
655	      version = "~> 3.5"
656	    }
657	    null = {
658	      source  = "hashicorp/null"
659	      version = "~> 3.2"
660	    }
661	  }
662	
663	  # Backend configuration (optional here, often in backend.tf)
664	  backend "s3" {
665	    bucket = "my-terraform-state"
666	    key    = "infrastructure/terraform.tfstate"
667	    region = "us-east-1"
668	  }
669	}
670	```
671	
672	---
673	
674	## Refactoring Patterns
675	
676	### Terraform Version Upgrades
677	
678	#### 0.12/0.13 → 1.x Migration Checklist
679	
680	**Replace legacy patterns with modern equivalents:**
681	
682	- [ ] Replace `element(concat(...))` with `try()`
683	- [ ] Add `nullable = false` to variables that shouldn't accept null
684	- [ ] Use `optional()` in object types for optional attributes
685	- [ ] Add `validation` blocks to variables with constraints
686	- [ ] Migrate secrets to write-only arguments (Terraform 1.11+)
687	- [ ] Use `moved` blocks for resource refactoring (Terraform 1.1+)
688	- [ ] Consider cross-variable validation (Terraform 1.9+)
689	
690	**Example migration:**
691	
692	```hcl
693	# Before (0.12 style)
694	output "security_group_id" {
695	  value = element(concat(aws_security_group.this.*.id, [""]), 0)
696	}
697	
698	variable "config" {
699	  type = object({
700	    name = string
701	    size = number
702	  })
703	}
704	
705	# After (1.x style)
706	output "security_group_id" {
707	  description = "The ID of the security group"
708	  value       = try(aws_security_group.this[0].id, "")
709	}
710	
711	variable "config" {
712	  description = "Configuration settings"
713	  type = object({
714	    name = string
715	    size = optional(number, 100)  # Optional with default
716	  })
717	  nullable = false  # Don't accept null
718	}
719	```
720	
721	### Secrets Remediation
722	
723	**Pattern:** Move secrets out of Terraform state into external secret management.
724	
725	#### Before - Secrets in State
726	
727	```hcl
728	# ❌ BAD - Secret generated and stored in state
729	resource "random_password" "db" {
730	  length  = 16
731	  special = true
732	}
733	
734	resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
735	  engine   = "mysql"
736	  username = "admin"
737	  password = random_password.db.result  # In state!
738	}
739	
740	# OR
741	
742	# ❌ BAD - Secret passed via variable and stored in state
743	variable "db_password" {
744	  description = "Database password"
745	  type        = string
746	  sensitive   = true  # Marked sensitive but still in state!
747	}
748	
749	resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
750	  password = var.db_password  # In state!
751	}
752	```
753	
754	#### After - External Secret Management
755	
756	**Option 1: Write-only arguments (Terraform 1.11+)**
757	
758	```hcl
759	# ✅ GOOD - Fetch from AWS Secrets Manager
760	data "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_password" {
761	  name = "prod-database-password"
762	}
763	
764	data "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password" {
765	  secret_id = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password.id
766	}
767	
768	resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
769	  engine   = "mysql"
770	  username = "admin"
771	
772	  # write-only: Sent to AWS, not stored in state
773	  password_wo = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret_version.db_password.secret_string
774	}
775	```
776	
777	**Option 2: Separate secret creation (if Terraform 1.11+ not available)**
778	
779	```hcl
780	# ✅ GOOD - Reference pre-existing secret
781	# Secret created outside Terraform (manually or separate process)
782	
783	data "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_password" {
784	  name = "prod-database-password"
785	}
786	
787	data "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password" {
788	  secret_id = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password.id
789	}
790	
791	# Note: Without write-only, you may need to handle secret rotation
792	# outside Terraform or accept that the secret value appears in state
793	# during initial creation but not after rotation
794	```
795	
796	**Migration steps:**
797	
798	1. Create secret in AWS Secrets Manager (outside Terraform)
799	2. Update Terraform to use data sources
800	3. Use write-only argument (if Terraform 1.11+)
801	4. Remove `random_password` resource or variable
802	5. Run `terraform apply` to update
803	6. Verify secret not in state: `terraform show` should not display password
804	
805	---
806	
807	## Locals for Dependency Management
808	
809	**Use locals to hint explicit resource deletion order:**
810	
811	```hcl
812	# ✅ GOOD - Forces correct deletion order
813	# Ensures subnets deleted before secondary CIDR blocks
814	
815	locals {
816	  # References secondary CIDR first, falling back to VPC
817	  # This forces Terraform to delete subnets before CIDR association
818	  vpc_id = try(
819	    aws_vpc_ipv4_cidr_block_association.this[0].vpc_id,
820	    aws_vpc.this.id,
821	    ""
822	  )
823	}
824	
825	resource "aws_vpc" "this" {
826	  cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16"
827	}
828	
829	resource "aws_vpc_ipv4_cidr_block_association" "this" {
830	  count = var.add_secondary_cidr ? 1 : 0
831	
832	  vpc_id     = aws_vpc.this.id
833	  cidr_block = "10.1.0.0/16"
834	}
835	
836	resource "aws_subnet" "public" {
837	  # Uses local instead of direct reference
838	  # Creates implicit dependency on CIDR association
839	  vpc_id     = local.vpc_id
840	  cidr_block = "10.1.0.0/24"
841	}
842	
843	# Without local: Terraform might try to delete CIDR before subnets → ERROR
844	# With local: Subnets deleted first, then CIDR association, then VPC ✓
845	```
846	
847	**Why this matters:**
848	- Prevents deletion errors when destroying infrastructure
849	- Ensures correct dependency order without explicit `depends_on`
850	- Particularly useful for complex VPC configurations with secondary CIDR blocks
851	
852	**Common use cases:**
853	- VPC with secondary CIDR blocks
854	- Resources that depend on optional configurations
855	- Complex deletion order requirements
856	
857	---
858	
859	**Back to:** [Main Skill File](../SKILL.md)
860

file.content:
# Code Patterns & Structure

> **Part of:** [terraform-skill](../SKILL.md)
> **Purpose:** Comprehensive patterns for Terraform/OpenTofu code structure and modern features

This document provides detailed code patterns, structure guidelines, and modern Terraform features. For high-level principles, see the [main skill file](../SKILL.md).

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Block Ordering & Structure](#block-ordering--structure)
2. [Count vs For_Each Deep Dive](#count-vs-for_each-deep-dive)
3. [Modern Terraform Features (1.0+)](#modern-terraform-features-10)
4. [Version Management](#version-management)
5. [Refactoring Patterns](#refactoring-patterns)
6. [Locals for Dependency Management](#locals-for-dependency-management)

---

## Block Ordering & Structure

### Resource Block Structure

**Strict argument ordering:**

1. `count` or `for_each` FIRST (blank line after)
2. Other arguments (alphabetical or logical grouping)
3. `tags` as last real argument
4. `depends_on` after tags (if needed)
5. `lifecycle` at the very end (if needed)

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Correct ordering
resource "aws_nat_gateway" "this" {
  count = var.create_nat_gateway ? 1 : 0

  allocation_id = aws_eip.this[0].id
  subnet_id     = aws_subnet.public[0].id

  tags = {
    Name        = "${var.name}-nat"
    Environment = var.environment
  }

  depends_on = [aws_internet_gateway.this]

  lifecycle {
    create_before_destroy = true
  }
}

# ❌ BAD - Wrong ordering
resource "aws_nat_gateway" "this" {
  allocation_id = aws_eip.this[0].id

  tags = { Name = "nat" }

  count = var.create_nat_gateway ? 1 : 0  # Should be first

  subnet_id = aws_subnet.public[0].id

  lifecycle {
    create_before_destroy = true
  }

  depends_on = [aws_internet_gateway.this]  # Should be after tags
}
```

### Variable Definition Structure

**Variable block ordering:**

1. `description` (ALWAYS required)
2. `type`
3. `default`
4. `sensitive` (when setting to true)
5. `nullable` (when setting to false)
6. `validation`

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Correct ordering and structure
variable "environment" {
  description = "Environment name for resource tagging"
  type        = string
  default     = "dev"
  nullable    = false

  validation {
    condition     = contains(["dev", "staging", "prod"], var.environment)
    error_message = "Environment must be one of: dev, staging, prod."
  }
}
```

### Variable Type Preferences

- Prefer **simple types** (`string`, `number`, `list()`, `map()`) over `object()` unless strict validation needed
- Use `optional()` for optional object attributes (Terraform 1.3+)
- Use `any` to disable validation at certain depths or support multiple types

**Modern variable patterns (Terraform 1.3+):**

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Using optional() for object attributes
variable "database_config" {
  description = "Database configuration with optional parameters"
  type = object({
    name               = string
    engine             = string
    instance_class     = string
    backup_retention   = optional(number, 7)      # Default: 7
    monitoring_enabled = optional(bool, true)     # Default: true
    tags               = optional(map(string), {}) # Default: {}
  })
}

# Usage - only required fields needed
database_config = {
  name           = "mydb"
  engine         = "mysql"
  instance_class = "db.t3.micro"
  # Optional fields use defaults
}
```

**Complex type example:**

```hcl
# For lists/maps of same type
variable "subnet_configs" {
  description = "Map of subnet configurations"
  type        = map(map(string))  # All values are maps of strings
}

# When types vary, use any
variable "mixed_config" {
  description = "Configuration with varying types"
  type        = any
}
```

### Output Structure

**Pattern:** `{name}_{type}_{attribute}`

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD
output "security_group_id" {  # "this_" should be omitted
  description = "The ID of the security group"
  value       = try(aws_security_group.this[0].id, "")
}

output "private_subnet_ids" {  # Plural for list
  description = "List of private subnet IDs"
  value       = aws_subnet.private[*].id
}

# ❌ BAD
output "this_security_group_id" {  # Don't prefix with "this_"
  value = aws_security_group.this[0].id
}

output "subnet_id" {  # Should be plural "subnet_ids"
  value = aws_subnet.private[*].id  # Returns list
}
```

---

## Count vs For_Each Deep Dive

### When to use count

✓ **Simple numeric replication:**
```hcl
resource "aws_subnet" "public" {
  count = 3

  cidr_block = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, count.index)
}
```

✓ **Boolean conditions (create or don't):**
```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Boolean condition
resource "aws_nat_gateway" "this" {
  count = var.create_nat_gateway ? 1 : 0
}

# Less preferred - length check
resource "aws_nat_gateway" "this" {
  count = length(var.public_subnets) > 0 ? 1 : 0
}
```

✓ **When order doesn't matter and items won't change**

### When to use for_each

✓ **Reference resources by key:**
```hcl
resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
  for_each = toset(var.availability_zones)

  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.this.id
  availability_zone = each.key
  cidr_block        = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 4, index(var.availability_zones, each.key))
}

# Reference by key: aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"]
```

✓ **Items may be added/removed from middle:**
```hcl
# ❌ BAD with count - removing middle item recreates all subsequent resources
resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
  count = length(var.availability_zones)

  availability_zone = var.availability_zones[count.index]
  # If var.availability_zones[1] removed, all resources after recreated!
}

# ✅ GOOD with for_each - removal only affects that one resource
resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
  for_each = toset(var.availability_zones)

  availability_zone = each.key
  # Removing one AZ only destroys that subnet
}
```

✓ **Creating multiple named resources:**
```hcl
variable "environments" {
  default = {
    dev = {
      instance_type = "t3.micro"
      instance_count = 1
    }
    prod = {
      instance_type = "t3.large"
      instance_count = 3
    }
  }
}

resource "aws_instance" "app" {
  for_each = var.environments

  instance_type = each.value.instance_type
  count         = each.value.instance_count

  tags = {
    Environment = each.key  # "dev" or "prod"
  }
}
```

### Count to For_Each Migration

**When to migrate:** When you need stable resource addressing or items might be added/removed from middle of list.

**Migration steps:**

1. Add `for_each` to resource
2. Use `moved` blocks to preserve existing resources
3. Remove `count` after verifying with `terraform plan`

**Complete example:**

```hcl
# Before (using count)
variable "availability_zones" {
  default = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"]
}

resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
  count = length(var.availability_zones)

  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.this.id
  cidr_block        = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, count.index)
  availability_zone = var.availability_zones[count.index]

  tags = {
    Name = "private-${var.availability_zones[count.index]}"
  }
}

# Reference: aws_subnet.private[0].id

# After (using for_each)
resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
  for_each = toset(var.availability_zones)

  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.this.id
  cidr_block        = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, index(var.availability_zones, each.key))
  availability_zone = each.key

  tags = {
    Name = "private-${each.key}"
  }
}

# Reference: aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"].id

# Migration blocks (prevents resource recreation)
moved {
  from = aws_subnet.private[0]
  to   = aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"]
}

moved {
  from = aws_subnet.private[1]
  to   = aws_subnet.private["us-east-1b"]
}

moved {
  from = aws_subnet.private[2]
  to   = aws_subnet.private["us-east-1c"]
}

# Verify migration:
# terraform plan should show "moved" operations, not destroy/create
```

**Benefits after migration:**
- Removing "us-east-1b" only destroys that subnet (not c)
- Adding new AZ doesn't affect existing subnets
- Resources have stable addresses by AZ name

---

## Modern Terraform Features (1.0+)

### try() Function (Terraform 0.13+)

**Use try() instead of element(concat()):**

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Modern try() function
output "security_group_id" {
  description = "The ID of the security group"
  value       = try(aws_security_group.this[0].id, "")
}

output "first_subnet_id" {
  description = "ID of first subnet with multiple fallbacks"
  value       = try(
    aws_subnet.public[0].id,
    aws_subnet.private[0].id,
    ""
  )
}

# ❌ BAD - Legacy pattern
output "security_group_id" {
  value = element(concat(aws_security_group.this.*.id, [""]), 0)
}
```

### nullable = false (Terraform 1.1+)

**Set nullable = false for non-null variables:**

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD (Terraform 1.1+)
variable "vpc_cidr" {
  description = "CIDR block for VPC"
  type        = string
  nullable    = false  # Passing null uses default, not null
  default     = "10.0.0.0/16"
}
```

### optional() with Defaults (Terraform 1.3+)

**Use optional() for object attributes:**

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Using optional() for object attributes
variable "database_config" {
  description = "Database configuration with optional parameters"
  type = object({
    name               = string
    engine             = string
    instance_class     = string
    backup_retention   = optional(number, 7)      # Default: 7
    monitoring_enabled = optional(bool, true)     # Default: true
    tags               = optional(map(string), {}) # Default: {}
  })
}

# Usage - only required fields needed
database_config = {
  name           = "mydb"
  engine         = "mysql"
  instance_class = "db.t3.micro"
  # Optional fields use defaults
}
```

### Moved Blocks (Terraform 1.1+)

**Rename resources without destroy/recreate:**

```hcl
# Rename a resource
moved {
  from = aws_instance.web_server
  to   = aws_instance.web
}

# Rename a module
moved {
  from = module.old_module_name
  to   = module.new_module_name
}

# Move resource into for_each
moved {
  from = aws_subnet.private[0]
  to   = aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"]
}
```

### Provider-Defined Functions (Terraform 1.8+)

**Use provider-specific functions for data transformation:**

```hcl
# AWS provider function example
data "aws_region" "current" {}

locals {
  # Provider function (Terraform 1.8+)
  bucket_name = provider::aws::arn_build("s3", "my-bucket", data.aws_region.current.name)
}

# Check provider documentation for available functions
# Common providers adding functions: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud
```

### Cross-Variable Validation (Terraform 1.9+)

**Reference other variables in validation blocks:**

```hcl
variable "instance_type" {
  description = "EC2 instance type"
  type        = string
}

variable "storage_size" {
  description = "Storage size in GB"
  type        = number

  validation {
    # Can reference var.instance_type in Terraform 1.9+
    condition = !(
      var.instance_type == "db.t3.micro" &&
      var.storage_size > 1000
    )
    error_message = "Micro instances cannot have storage > 1000 GB"
  }
}

variable "environment" {
  description = "Environment name"
  type        = string
}

variable "backup_retention" {
  description = "Backup retention period in days"
  type        = number

  validation {
    # Production requires longer retention
    condition = (
      var.environment == "prod" ? var.backup_retention >= 7 : true
    )
    error_message = "Production environment requires backup_retention >= 7 days"
  }
}
```

### Write-Only Arguments (Terraform 1.11+)

**Always use write-only arguments or external secret management:**

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - External secret with write-only argument
data "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_password" {
  name = "prod-database-password"
}

data "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password" {
  secret_id = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password.id
}

resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
  engine         = "mysql"
  instance_class = "db.t3.micro"
  username       = "admin"

  # write-only: Terraform sends to AWS then forgets it (not in state)
  password_wo = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret_version.db_password.secret_string
}

# ❌ BAD - Secret ends up in state file
resource "random_password" "db" {
  length = 16
}

resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
  password = random_password.db.result  # Stored in state!
}

# ❌ BAD - Variable secret stored in state
resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
  password = var.db_password  # Ends up in state file
}
```

---

## Version Management

### Version Constraint Syntax

```hcl
# Exact version (avoid unless necessary - inflexible)
version = "5.0.0"

# Pessimistic constraint (recommended for stability)
# Allows patch updates only
version = "~> 5.0"      # Allows 5.0.x (any x), but not 5.1.0
version = "~> 5.0.1"    # Allows 5.0.x where x >= 1, but not 5.1.0

# Range constraints
version = ">= 5.0, < 6.0"     # Any 5.x version
version = ">= 5.0.0, < 5.1.0" # Specific minor version range

# Minimum version
version = ">= 5.0"  # Any version 5.0 or higher (risky - breaking changes)

# Latest (avoid in production - unpredictable)
# No version specified = always use latest available
```

### Versioning Strategy by Component

**Terraform itself:**
```hcl
# versions.tf
terraform {
  # Pin to minor version, allow patch updates
  required_version = "~> 1.9"  # Allows 1.9.x
}
```

**Providers:**
```hcl
# versions.tf
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"  # Pin major version, allow minor/patch updates
    }
    random = {
      source  = "hashicorp/random"
      version = "~> 3.5"
    }
  }
}
```

**Modules:**
```hcl
# Production - pin exact version
module "vpc" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
  version = "5.1.2"  # Exact version for production stability
}

# Development - allow flexibility
module "vpc" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
  version = "~> 5.1"  # Allow patch updates in dev
}
```

### Update Strategy

**Security patches:**
- Update immediately
- Test in dev → stage → prod
- Prioritize provider and Terraform core updates

**Minor versions:**
- Regular maintenance windows (monthly/quarterly)
- Review changelog for breaking changes
- Test thoroughly before production

**Major versions:**
- Planned upgrade cycles
- Dedicated testing period
- May require code changes
- Update in phases: dev → stage → prod

### Version Management Workflow

```hcl
# Step 1: Lock versions in versions.tf
terraform {
  required_version = "~> 1.9"

  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
  }
}

# Step 2: Generate lock file (commit this)
terraform init
# Creates .terraform.lock.hcl with exact versions used

# Step 3: Update providers when needed
terraform init -upgrade
# Updates to latest within constraints

# Step 4: Review and test changes before committing
terraform plan
```

### Example versions.tf Template

```hcl
terraform {
  # Terraform version
  required_version = "~> 1.9"

  # Provider versions
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
    random = {
      source  = "hashicorp/random"
      version = "~> 3.5"
    }
    null = {
      source  = "hashicorp/null"
      version = "~> 3.2"
    }
  }

  # Backend configuration (optional here, often in backend.tf)
  backend "s3" {
    bucket = "my-terraform-state"
    key    = "infrastructure/terraform.tfstate"
    region = "us-east-1"
  }
}
```

---

## Refactoring Patterns

### Terraform Version Upgrades

#### 0.12/0.13 → 1.x Migration Checklist

**Replace legacy patterns with modern equivalents:**

- [ ] Replace `element(concat(...))` with `try()`
- [ ] Add `nullable = false` to variables that shouldn't accept null
- [ ] Use `optional()` in object types for optional attributes
- [ ] Add `validation` blocks to variables with constraints
- [ ] Migrate secrets to write-only arguments (Terraform 1.11+)
- [ ] Use `moved` blocks for resource refactoring (Terraform 1.1+)
- [ ] Consider cross-variable validation (Terraform 1.9+)

**Example migration:**

```hcl
# Before (0.12 style)
output "security_group_id" {
  value = element(concat(aws_security_group.this.*.id, [""]), 0)
}

variable "config" {
  type = object({
    name = string
    size = number
  })
}

# After (1.x style)
output "security_group_id" {
  description = "The ID of the security group"
  value       = try(aws_security_group.this[0].id, "")
}

variable "config" {
  description = "Configuration settings"
  type = object({
    name = string
    size = optional(number, 100)  # Optional with default
  })
  nullable = false  # Don't accept null
}
```

### Secrets Remediation

**Pattern:** Move secrets out of Terraform state into external secret management.

#### Before - Secrets in State

```hcl
# ❌ BAD - Secret generated and stored in state
resource "random_password" "db" {
  length  = 16
  special = true
}

resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
  engine   = "mysql"
  username = "admin"
  password = random_password.db.result  # In state!
}

# OR

# ❌ BAD - Secret passed via variable and stored in state
variable "db_password" {
  description = "Database password"
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true  # Marked sensitive but still in state!
}

resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
  password = var.db_password  # In state!
}
```

#### After - External Secret Management

**Option 1: Write-only arguments (Terraform 1.11+)**

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Fetch from AWS Secrets Manager
data "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_password" {
  name = "prod-database-password"
}

data "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password" {
  secret_id = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password.id
}

resource "aws_db_instance" "this" {
  engine   = "mysql"
  username = "admin"

  # write-only: Sent to AWS, not stored in state
  password_wo = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret_version.db_password.secret_string
}
```

**Option 2: Separate secret creation (if Terraform 1.11+ not available)**

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Reference pre-existing secret
# Secret created outside Terraform (manually or separate process)

data "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_password" {
  name = "prod-database-password"
}

data "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password" {
  secret_id = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password.id
}

# Note: Without write-only, you may need to handle secret rotation
# outside Terraform or accept that the secret value appears in state
# during initial creation but not after rotation
```

**Migration steps:**

1. Create secret in AWS Secrets Manager (outside Terraform)
2. Update Terraform to use data sources
3. Use write-only argument (if Terraform 1.11+)
4. Remove `random_password` resource or variable
5. Run `terraform apply` to update
6. Verify secret not in state: `terraform show` should not display password

---

## Locals for Dependency Management

**Use locals to hint explicit resource deletion order:**

```hcl
# ✅ GOOD - Forces correct deletion order
# Ensures subnets deleted before secondary CIDR blocks

locals {
  # References secondary CIDR first, falling back to VPC
  # This forces Terraform to delete subnets before CIDR association
  vpc_id = try(
    aws_vpc_ipv4_cidr_block_association.this[0].vpc_id,
    aws_vpc.this.id,
    ""
  )
}

resource "aws_vpc" "this" {
  cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16"
}

resource "aws_vpc_ipv4_cidr_block_association" "this" {
  count = var.add_secondary_cidr ? 1 : 0

  vpc_id     = aws_vpc.this.id
  cidr_block = "10.1.0.0/16"
}

resource "aws_subnet" "public" {
  # Uses local instead of direct reference
  # Creates implicit dependency on CIDR association
  vpc_id     = local.vpc_id
  cidr_block = "10.1.0.0/24"
}

# Without local: Terraform might try to delete CIDR before subnets → ERROR
# With local: Subnets deleted first, then CIDR association, then VPC ✓
```

**Why this matters:**
- Prevents deletion errors when destroying infrastructure
- Ensures correct dependency order without explicit `depends_on`
- Particularly useful for complex VPC configurations with secondary CIDR blocks

**Common use cases:**
- VPC with secondary CIDR blocks
- Resources that depend on optional configurations
- Complex deletion order requirements

---

content.text:
Scan complete. The real trivy findings (no HIGH/CRITICAL anywhere — confirms why CI is green):

| Finding | Sev | Where | In low-noise scope? |
|---|---|---|---|
| AWS-0065 KMS key rotation off | MED | both KMS keys | ✅ 1-liner |
| AWS-0090 S3 versioning off | MED | both s3 modules | ✅ small resource |
| AWS-0178 VPC flow logs off | MED | all 4 VPCs | ❌ needs IAM role + log group |
| AWS-0089 S3 access logging off | LOW | both s3 modules | ❌ needs log bucket |

Two corrections to my earlier plan, now that I've run the scanner + read the code-patterns/module-patterns refs:

- **`default-SG deny-all` and `abort-incomplete-multipart` are NOT trivy findings** (those are checkov-only rules). Since your CI gate is trivy, they'd be unverified churn → demoting to optional.
- **`deletion_window_in_days = 7` is already the AWS minimum** and has no trivy rule → comment-only, no code change.

So the scanner-verified, in-scope fixes are just **KMS rotation + S3 versioning**. Flow logs (MED) and S3 logging (LOW) fall outside "low-noise" (need IAM/log-bucket) → they stay as report-only findings under your MEDIUM-logging gate; I'll document them as a conscious teaching-scope gap. Code-patterns review surfaced no structural issues beyond the already-planned `nullable=false` + validation guardrails (subnets are singletons, so no count/for_each concern).

Updating the plan to match the evidence:

content.content:
24	- [ ] **Input validation guardrails** across `variables.tf`:
25	  - `region` -> `can(regex("^[a-z]{2}-[a-z]+-[0-9]$", var.region))`
26	  - `vpc_cidr` -> `can(cidrhost(var.vpc_cidr, 0))`
27	  - `environment` -> lowercase alnum/hyphen, 1-20 chars (keeps "dev/prod/etc." flexibility)
28	  - `bucket_prefix` -> S3-name-safe regex + length
29	  - add `nullable = false` to required non-null vars
30	
31	### B. CI gate (enforce the baseline)
32	- [ ] `.github/workflows/terraform.yml`: keep the existing trivy step (HIGH,CRITICAL, `exit-code: 1`); add a second trivy step `severity: MEDIUM`, `exit-code: 0` (report-only) so new MEDIUM findings surface without blocking.
33	
34	### C. Supply-chain (kept, secondary)
35	- [ ] Pin TFLint binary in `tf-setup` action (`tflint_version: latest` -> pinned).
36	- [ ] Add `terraform` ecosystem to `dependabot.yml` over `examples/**` (aws pinned at 6.41.0 has no auto-update path).
37	- [ ] _(optional)_ Commit `.terraform.lock.hcl` per stable root; gitleaks secret-scan job.
38	
39	### D. Docs (kept)
40	- [ ] Root `README.md` + `AGENTS.md`: note the security baseline (default-SG deny-all, KMS rotation, S3 versioning, input validation) and the MEDIUM-report-only gate.
41	- [ ] `tasks/lessons.md`: record "harden but keep examples minimal" convention + trivy gate policy.
42	
43	### Validation
44	`terraform fmt -check -recursive`; per touched root `terraform init -backend=false` + `terraform validate`; `tflint`. (terraform 1.14.8 + tflint available locally; alpha `02-after` validate needs the alpha CLI - fmt/validate-skip noted.)
45	
46	## History
47	
48	- 2026-06-12: Cut release `v2.0.0` (PR #11). Release PR was `BLOCKED` by the new branch protection (no `Semantic PR title` check, see Active); unblocked one-off by editing the PR body to retrigger the workflow, then squash-merged.
49	- 2026-06-11: Enable branch protection on `main`: require `Semantic PR title`, linear history, no force-push/deletion, conversation resolution; `enforce_admins` off (solo maintainer override), no required reviews.
50	- 2026-06-11: Skip per-commit commitlint on bot branches (`dependabot[bot]`, `release-please--*`) - PR #14, merged. Bot commit bodies are machine-generated and discarded on squash; the conventional-commit contract on `main` is the PR title, already enforced by `Semantic PR title`. Unblocked dependabot #9.
51	- 2026-06-11: Merge dependabot bumps: actions/checkout 6.0.3 (#9), googleapis/release-please-action 5.0.0 (#8), aquasecurity/trivy-action 0.36.0 (#7).
52	- 2026-06-11 — Restructure repo into a Terraform examples library (`examples/<name>/`, CI auto-discovery, taxonomy) — PR #10, merged
53	- 2026-06-11 — Add `terraform-deferred-actions` example: KMS + generic S3 module, native deferral demo — PR #12
54	- 2026-06-11 — Remodel `terraform-deferred-actions` into a before/after comparison (1.15.6 workaround vs 1.16 native); drop the managed-key bucket — PR #12
55	- 2026-06-11 — Fix deferred-actions README: `-allow-deferral` flag IS required on 1.16-alpha (verified via CLI); experiments block is not

  - `region` -> `can(regex("^[a-z]{2}-[a-z]+-[0-9]$", var.region))`
  - `vpc_cidr` -> `can(cidrhost(var.vpc_cidr, 0))`
  - `environment` -> lowercase alnum/hyphen, 1-20 chars (keeps "dev/prod/etc." flexibility)
  - `bucket_prefix` -> S3-name-safe regex + length
  - add `nullable = false` to required non-null vars

### B. CI gate (enforce the baseline)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/terraform.yml`: keep the existing trivy step (HIGH,CRITICAL, `exit-code: 1`); add a second trivy step `severity: MEDIUM`, `exit-code: 0` (report-only) so new MEDIUM findings surface without blocking.

### C. Supply-chain (kept, secondary)
- [ ] Pin TFLint binary in `tf-setup` action (`tflint_version: latest` -> pinned).
- [ ] Add `terraform` ecosystem to `dependabot.yml` over `examples/**` (aws pinned at 6.41.0 has no auto-update path).
- [ ] _(optional)_ Commit `.terraform.lock.hcl` per stable root; gitleaks secret-scan job.

### D. Docs (kept)
- [ ] Root `README.md` + `AGENTS.md`: note the security baseline (default-SG deny-all, KMS rotation, S3 versioning, input validation) and the MEDIUM-report-only gate.
- [ ] `tasks/lessons.md`: record "harden but keep examples minimal" convention + trivy gate policy.

### Validation
`terraform fmt -check -recursive`; per touched root `terraform init -backend=false` + `terraform validate`; `tflint`. (terraform 1.14.8 + tflint available locally; alpha `02-after` validate needs the alpha CLI - fmt/validate-skip noted.)

## History

- 2026-06-12: Cut release `v2.0.0` (PR #11). Release PR was `BLOCKED` by the new branch protection (no `Semantic PR title` check, see Active); unblocked one-off by editing the PR body to retrigger the workflow, then squash-merged.
- 2026-06-11: Enable branch protection on `main`: require `Semantic PR title`, linear history, no force-push/deletion, conversation resolution; `enforce_admins` off (solo maintainer override), no required reviews.
- 2026-06-11: Skip per-commit commitlint on bot branches (`dependabot[bot]`, `release-please--*`) - PR #14, merged. Bot commit bodies are machine-generated and discarded on squash; the conventional-commit contract on `main` is the PR title, already enforced by `Semantic PR title`. Unblocked dependabot #9.
- 2026-06-11: Merge dependabot bumps: actions/checkout 6.0.3 (#9), googleapis/release-please-action 5.0.0 (#8), aquasecurity/trivy-action 0.36.0 (#7).
- 2026-06-11 — Restructure repo into a Terraform examples library (`examples/<name>/`, CI auto-discovery, taxonomy) — PR #10, merged
- 2026-06-11 — Add `terraform-deferred-actions` example: KMS + generic S3 module, native deferral demo — PR #12
- 2026-06-11 — Remodel `terraform-deferred-actions` into a before/after comparison (1.15.6 workaround vs 1.16 native); drop the managed-key bucket — PR #12
- 2026-06-11 — Fix deferred-actions README: `-allow-deferral` flag IS required on 1.16-alpha (verified via CLI); experiments block is not

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# todo

## Active

Repo consistency to land before closing the deferred-actions live project:

- [ ] **Fix release-please PR deadlock under branch protection** (introduced 2026-06-11 when `main` protection made `Semantic PR title` a required check). Release PRs are created via `GITHUB_TOKEN`, which does not trigger workflows, so `pr-title.yml` never runs, the required check stays absent, and the PR is `BLOCKED`. Every future release PR is affected. Chosen fix: run release-please under a non-`GITHUB_TOKEN` identity (fine-grained PAT). See `lessons.md`.
  - [ ] _(owner: maintainer)_ create a fine-grained PAT scoped to `terraform-examples` only, perms `Contents: RW` + `Pull requests: RW`; store as repo secret `RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN`.
  - [ ] _(owner: agent)_ add `token: ${{ secrets.RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN }}` to the release-please step in `.github/workflows/release.yml` (own PR, conventional title).
  - [ ] verify on the next release cycle: release PR shows `Semantic PR title` green and is not `BLOCKED`.
- [ ] **Follow-up, unblocked by the PAT fix: make terraform CI required.** Today `validate + lint + scan` and `terraform fmt` are not required checks (dynamic matrix names + skip on non-IaC PRs, so a required-but-never-reported check would block forever). Add a stable "all-green" gate job (`needs:` the full matrix) and set it as the required status check on `main`.

## Hardening pass (terraform-skill review, 2026-06-13)

Focus: harden the Terraform code itself. CI is green today => no HIGH/CRITICAL findings; all gaps are MEDIUM/best-practice. Scope = **low-noise wins only** (no new IAM roles / log buckets - they'd obscure the minimal teaching examples). Gate policy = keep HIGH,CRITICAL blocking, add MEDIUM report-only.

### A. TF code hardening (primary)
- [ ] **KMS hardening** in deferred-actions `01-before/main.tf` and `02-after/main.tf` (`aws_kms_key.root`):
  - add `enable_key_rotation = true` (trivy AVD-AWS-0065 / checkov CKV_AWS_7)
  - keep `deletion_window_in_days = 7` (already at the AWS 7-day minimum) and add a comment documenting intent: fastest teardown, no lingering pending-deletion keys / cost in a demo
- [ ] **S3 versioning** - add `aws_s3_bucket_versioning` (Enabled) to both `s3-bucket-encrypted` modules. (CKV_AWS_21)
- [ ] **S3 abort incomplete multipart** - add `aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration` (empty `filter {}` + `abort_incomplete_multipart_upload { days_after_initiation = 7 }`) to both modules. (CKV_AWS_300)
- [ ] **VPC default-SG lockdown** - add `aws_default_security_group "this"` (no ingress/egress = deny-all) in the 4 VPC-owning roots/modules: level-0, level-1, level-2 network module, level-3 network module. (CKV2_AWS_12)
- [ ] **Input validation guardrails** across `variables.tf`:
  - `region` -> `can(regex("^[a-z]{2}-[a-z]+-[0-9]$", var.region))`
  - `vpc_cidr` -> `can(cidrhost(var.vpc_cidr, 0))`
  - `environment` -> lowercase alnum/hyphen, 1-20 chars (keeps "dev/prod/etc." flexibility)
  - `bucket_prefix` -> S3-name-safe regex + length
  - add `nullable = false` to required non-null vars

### B. CI gate (enforce the baseline)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/terraform.yml`: keep the existing trivy step (HIGH,CRITICAL, `exit-code: 1`); add a second trivy step `severity: MEDIUM`, `exit-code: 0` (report-only) so new MEDIUM findings surface without blocking.

### C. Supply-chain (kept, secondary)
- [ ] Pin TFLint binary in `tf-setup` action (`tflint_version: latest` -> pinned).
- [ ] Add `terraform` ecosystem to `dependabot.yml` over `examples/**` (aws pinned at 6.41.0 has no auto-update path).
- [ ] _(optional)_ Commit `.terraform.lock.hcl` per stable root; gitleaks secret-scan job.

### D. Docs (kept)
- [ ] Root `README.md` + `AGENTS.md`: note the security baseline (default-SG deny-all, KMS rotation, S3 versioning, input validation) and the MEDIUM-report-only gate.
- [ ] `tasks/lessons.md`: record "harden but keep examples minimal" convention + trivy gate policy.

### Validation
`terraform fmt -check -recursive`; per touched root `terraform init -backend=false` + `terraform validate`; `tflint`. (terraform 1.14.8 + tflint available locally; alpha `02-after` validate needs the alpha CLI - fmt/validate-skip noted.)

## History

- 2026-06-12: Cut release `v2.0.0` (PR #11). Release PR was `BLOCKED` by the new branch protection (no `Semantic PR title` check, see Active); unblocked one-off by editing the PR body to retrigger the workflow, then squash-merged.
- 2026-06-11: Enable branch protection on `main`: require `Semantic PR title`, linear history, no force-push/deletion, conversation resolution; `enforce_admins` off (solo maintainer override), no required reviews.
- 2026-06-11: Skip per-commit commitlint on bot branches (`dependabot[bot]`, `release-please--*`) - PR #14, merged. Bot commit bodies are machine-generated and discarded on squash; the conventional-commit contract on `main` is the PR title, already enforced by `Semantic PR title`. Unblocked dependabot #9.
- 2026-06-11: Merge dependabot bumps: actions/checkout 6.0.3 (#9), googleapis/release-please-action 5.0.0 (#8), aquasecurity/trivy-action 0.36.0 (#7).
- 2026-06-11 — Restructure repo into a Terraform examples library (`examples/<name>/`, CI auto-discovery, taxonomy) — PR #10, merged
- 2026-06-11 — Add `terraform-deferred-actions` example: KMS + generic S3 module, native deferral demo — PR #12
- 2026-06-11 — Remodel `terraform-deferred-actions` into a before/after comparison (1.15.6 workaround vs 1.16 native); drop the managed-key bucket — PR #12
- 2026-06-11 — Fix deferred-actions README: `-allow-deferral` flag IS required on 1.16-alpha (verified via CLI); experiments block is not

content.text: Est-ce qu'on n'a pas moyen avec Trivi de faire en un seul job l'enforcement sur les High et les Critical et de simplement avoir un report sur les Medium pour éviter d'avoir les deux jobs qui consomment deux fois les ressources ? Et sinon, dégage tout simplement, comme on n'a pas fait de travaux, tout ce qui est relatif à Tchékov. Encore une fois, on garde les éléments à faire et on ne trace pas ce qui n'a pas lieu d'être.

Repo consistency to land before closing the deferred-actions live project:

- [ ] **Fix release-please PR deadlock under branch protection** (introduced 2026-06-11 when `main` protection made `Semantic PR title` a required check).   - [ ] _(owner: maintainer)_ create a fine-grained PAT scoped to `terraform-examples` only, perms `Contents: RW` + `Pull requests: RW`; store as repo secret `RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN`.
  - [ ] _(owner: agent)_ add `token: ${{ secrets.RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN }}` to the release-please step in `.github/workflows/release.yml` (own PR, conventional title).

Focus: harden the Terraform code itself, verified with `trivy config` (v0.71.1, run 2026-06-13). Scope = **low-noise wins only** (no new IAM roles / log buckets - they'd obscure the minimal teaching examples). Gate policy = keep HIGH,CRITICAL blocking, add MEDIUM report-only.

**Verified trivy findings (all severities; no HIGH/CRITICAL -> confirms CI green):**
- AWS-0065 (MED) KMS key rotation off - both `aws_kms_key.root` (01-before, 02-after)
- AWS-0090 (MED) S3 versioning off - both `s3-bucket-encrypted` modules
- AWS-0178 (MED) VPC flow logs off - all 4 VPCs (via network module x2 + level-0/1)
- AWS-0089 (LOW) S3 access logging off - both `s3-bucket-encrypted` modules

### A. TF code hardening (primary - scanner-verified, in scope)
- [ ] **KMS rotation** - add `enable_key_rotation = true` to `aws_kms_key.root` in `01-before/main.tf` + `02-after/main.tf`. Clears AWS-0065. Also add a one-line comment on `deletion_window_in_days = 7` (already the AWS minimum; documents demo-teardown intent - no scanner rule, no value change).
- [ ] **S3 versioning** - add `aws_s3_bucket_versioning` (Enabled) to both `s3-bucket-encrypted` modules. Clears AWS-0090.
- [ ] **Input validation guardrails** across `variables.tf` (skill best-practice; not scanner-detected):
  - `region` -> `can(regex("^[a-z]{2}-[a-z]+-[0-9]$", var.region))`
  - `vpc_cidr` -> `can(cidrhost(var.vpc_cidr, 0))`
  - `environment` -> lowercase alnum/hyphen, 1-20 chars (keeps "dev/prod/etc." flexibility)
  - `bucket_prefix` -> S3-name-safe regex + length
  - add `nullable = false` to required non-null vars

### B. CI gate (enforce the baseline)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/terraform.yml`: keep the existing trivy step (HIGH,CRITICAL, `exit-code: 1`); add a second trivy step `severity: MEDIUM`, `exit-code: 0` (report-only) so new MEDIUM findings surface without blocking.

### C. Supply-chain (kept, secondary)
- [ ] Pin TFLint binary in `tf-setup` action (`tflint_version: latest` -> pinned).
- [ ] Add `terraform` ecosystem to `dependabot.yml` over `examples/**` (aws pinned at 6.41.0 has no auto-update path).
- [ ] _(optional)_ Commit `.terraform.lock.hcl` per stable root; gitleaks secret-scan job.

### D. Docs (kept)
- [ ] Root `README.md` + `AGENTS.md`: note the security baseline (default-SG deny-all, KMS rotation, S3 versioning, input validation) and the MEDIUM-report-only gate.
- [ ] `tasks/lessons.md`: record "harden but keep examples minimal" convention + trivy gate policy.

### Validation
`terraform fmt -check -recursive`; per touched root `terraform init -backend=false` + `terraform validate`; `tflint`. (terraform 1.14.8 + tflint available locally; alpha `02-after` validate needs the alpha CLI - fmt/validate-skip noted.)

content.text: Bonne intuition. Oui, trivy peut scanner **une seule fois** puis ré-évaluer le rapport sans rescanner, via `trivy convert`. Je teste ça localement pour confirmer le comportement exit-code par sévérité :

input.command:
cd /Users/bgauduch/git/demo-terraform
d=examples/terraform-deferred-actions/01-before
# 1 seul scan -> JSON, non bloquant, toutes sévérités
trivy config --quiet --severity LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --format json --output /tmp/tf.json --exit-code 0 "$d" 2>/dev/null
echo "=== convert: report MEDIUM+ (non bloquant, exit attendu 0) ==="
trivy convert --format table --severity MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 0 /tmp/tf.json 2>/dev/null | tail -5
echo "exit=$?"
echo
echo "=== convert: enforce HIGH,CRITICAL (exit attendu 1 si trouvé, ici 0 car aucun) ==="
trivy convert --format table --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1 /tmp/tf.json 2>/dev/null | tail -3
echo "exit=$?"

exit=0

exit=0

content.text:
Parfait, confirmé : `trivy convert` ré-évalue le rapport JSON déjà produit avec sa propre `--severity` + `--exit-code`, **sans rescanner**, et l'exit-code 1 se déclenche bien. Donc un seul scan suffit.

Le pattern single-job (par cellule de la matrice) :
1. `trivy config … --format json --output report.json --exit-code 0` → **un seul scan**
2. `trivy convert --severity MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 0 report.json` → report visible, non bloquant
3. `trivy convert --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1 report.json` → gate bloquant H/C

Je mets à jour le plan : CI en single-scan + convert, et je dégage tout le checkov.

  - [ ] _(owner: maintainer)_ create a fine-grained PAT scoped to `terraform-examples` only, perms `Contents: RW` + `Pull requests: RW`; store as repo secret `RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN`.
  - [ ] _(owner: agent)_ add `token: ${{ secrets.RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN }}` to the release-please step in `.github/workflows/release.yml` (own PR, conventional title).
  - [ ] verify on the next release cycle: release PR shows `Semantic PR title` green and is not `BLOCKED`.
- [ ] **Follow-up, unblocked by the PAT fix: make terraform CI required.** Today `validate + lint + scan` and `terraform fmt` are not required checks (dynamic matrix names + skip on non-IaC PRs, so a required-but-never-reported check would block forever). Add a stable "all-green" gate job (`needs:` the full matrix) and set it as the required status check on `main`.

## Hardening pass (terraform-skill review, 2026-06-13)

Focus: harden the Terraform code itself, verified with `trivy config` (v0.71.1, run 2026-06-13). Scope = **low-noise wins only** (no new IAM roles / log buckets - they'd obscure the minimal teaching examples). Gate policy = keep HIGH,CRITICAL blocking, add MEDIUM report-only.

**Verified trivy findings (all severities; no HIGH/CRITICAL -> confirms CI green):**
- AWS-0065 (MED) KMS key rotation off - both `aws_kms_key.root` (01-before, 02-after)
- AWS-0090 (MED) S3 versioning off - both `s3-bucket-encrypted` modules
- AWS-0178 (MED) VPC flow logs off - all 4 VPCs (via network module x2 + level-0/1)
- AWS-0089 (LOW) S3 access logging off - both `s3-bucket-encrypted` modules

### A. TF code hardening (primary - scanner-verified, in scope)
- [ ] **KMS rotation** - add `enable_key_rotation = true` to `aws_kms_key.root` in `01-before/main.tf` + `02-after/main.tf`. Clears AWS-0065. Also add a one-line comment on `deletion_window_in_days = 7` (already the AWS minimum; documents demo-teardown intent - no scanner rule, no value change).
- [ ] **S3 versioning** - add `aws_s3_bucket_versioning` (Enabled) to both `s3-bucket-encrypted` modules. Clears AWS-0090.
- [ ] **Input validation guardrails** across `variables.tf` (skill best-practice; not scanner-detected):
  - `region` -> `can(regex("^[a-z]{2}-[a-z]+-[0-9]$", var.region))`
  - `vpc_cidr` -> `can(cidrhost(var.vpc_cidr, 0))`
  - `environment` -> lowercase alnum/hyphen, 1-20 chars (keeps "dev/prod/etc." flexibility)
  - `bucket_prefix` -> S3-name-safe regex + length
  - add `nullable = false` to required non-null vars (order per skill: description, type, default, nullable, validation)

### B. Out-of-scope findings (conscious teaching-scope gaps)
- [ ] **AWS-0178 VPC flow logs (MED)** + **AWS-0089 S3 logging (LOW)** - excluded: both need extra infra (IAM role + CloudWatch log group; second log bucket) that obscures the minimal examples. Remain visible as report-only under the MEDIUM gate. Document the rationale in `lessons.md` (and optionally a commented `.trivyignore`). Decide: leave reported (recommended, honest) vs `.trivyignore` to silence.

### C. CI gate (enforce the baseline - single scan, no double-spend)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/terraform.yml`: replace the single trivy step with **one scan + two converts** (verified locally, trivy 0.71.1 supports `convert` re-eval without rescanning):
  1. `trivy config <cell.dir> --severity LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --format json --output report.json --exit-code 0` (scan once, non-blocking)
  2. `trivy convert --format table --severity MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 0 report.json` (report, visible in logs)
  3. `trivy convert --format table --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1 report.json` (gate: fails only on HIGH/CRITICAL)
  - Needs trivy on PATH: install via pinned `aquasecurity/setup-trivy` (also pins the trivy version - supply-chain bonus) and run the three commands in one `run:` step, instead of the `trivy-action` wrapper.

### D. Supply-chain (kept, secondary)
- [ ] Pin TFLint binary in `tf-setup` action (`tflint_version: latest` -> pinned).
- [ ] Add `terraform` ecosystem to `dependabot.yml` over `examples/**` (aws pinned at 6.41.0 has no auto-update path).
- [ ] _(optional)_ Commit `.terraform.lock.hcl` per stable root; gitleaks secret-scan job.

### F. Docs (kept)
- [ ] Root `README.md` + `AGENTS.md`: note the security baseline (KMS rotation, S3 versioning, input validation) + the MEDIUM-report-only gate + the accepted flow-logs/logging gap.
- [ ] `tasks/lessons.md`: record "harden but keep examples minimal" convention, trivy-vs-checkov rule coverage, and the gate policy.

### Validation
`terraform fmt -check -recursive`; per touched root `terraform init -backend=false` + `terraform validate`; `tflint`. (terraform 1.14.8 + tflint available locally; alpha `02-after` validate needs the alpha CLI - fmt/validate-skip noted.)

## History

- 2026-06-12: Cut release `v2.0.0` (PR #11). Release PR was `BLOCKED` by the new branch protection (no `Semantic PR title` check, see Active); unblocked one-off by editing the PR body to retrigger the workflow, then squash-merged.
- 2026-06-11: Enable branch protection on `main`: require `Semantic PR title`, linear history, no force-push/deletion, conversation resolution; `enforce_admins` off (solo maintainer override), no required reviews.
- 2026-06-11: Skip per-commit commitlint on bot branches (`dependabot[bot]`, `release-please--*`) - PR #14, merged. Bot commit bodies are machine-generated and discarded on squash; the conventional-commit contract on `main` is the PR title, already enforced by `Semantic PR title`. Unblocked dependabot #9.
- 2026-06-11: Merge dependabot bumps: actions/checkout 6.0.3 (#9), googleapis/release-please-action 5.0.0 (#8), aquasecurity/trivy-action 0.36.0 (#7).

I'm setting up an autonomous, hands-off Claude Code session: the user wants to leave Claude running unattended to implement a set of tasks across multiple PRs, push branches, open PRs via gh, monitor GitHub Actions CI, fix failures, and keep iterating until everything is green — pinging the user back ONLY on a blocker or on completion.

Research the current Claude Code docs (use WebFetch on docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code and WebSearch as needed) and give me an accurate, current inventory of the features that enable this, with concrete usage. Specifically cover:

1. Permission modes for unattended runs: `acceptEdits`, `bypassPermissions`, `plan`, `default` — how to set them (CLI flags like --permission-mode, --dangerously-skip-permissions, settings.json), and the safety tradeoffs.
